title: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
body:
### Issue Description

```powershell
podman --version
podman.exe version 5.3.1
```

I am using remote client following this [tutorial](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/mac_win_client.md).
I used VScode to establish a SSH connection so it added an entry in the config file (C:\Users\UserName\.ssh\config) like this:
```
Host 222.222.222.222
  HostName 222.222.222.222
  User UserName
```
After I did all the tutorial said, I tried `podman ps`:
```powershell
PS > podman ps
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp :22: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
```
This problem was finally worked out as soon as I changed the config file entry as follows:
```
Host Server
  HostName 222.222.222.222
  User UserName
```
It seems that when resolving the URI, the ip string was recongnized as host name.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. follow this [tutorial](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/mac_win_client.md).
2. added an entry in the client config file (C:\Users\UserName\.ssh\config) like this:
```
Host 222.222.222.222
  HostName 222.222.222.222
  User UserName
```
3. run `podman ps` at the client host


### Describe the results you received

```powershell
PS > podman ps
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp :22: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
```

### Describe the results you expected

```powershell
PS > podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED      STATUS      PORTS                 NAMES
```

comments.body:
could you show the full command where you added the system connection?

totally unrelated: note, you are using a 5.3. podman client with a 4.X podman service which is not supported and could be dicey

Thanks for reply.

> could you show the full command where you added the system connection?

My command is like this: `podman system connection add <MACHINE_NAME> --identity "C:\Users\UserName\.ssh\id_ed25519" ssh://user@/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock` as I am running the default user on the Ubuntu server.

> totally unrelated: note, you are using a 5.3. podman client with a 4.X podman service which is not supported and could be dicey

I used `apt install podman` to install podman so the version is a bit old.
If I want to install the 5.3 version podman on ubuntu, is there any other way than building from source?

I can reproduce this on 5.5.2

The situation seems to be as follows:

vscode adds a `.ssh/config` entry like:
```
Host foo
    HostName foo
```
This then breaks the host lookup in podman's ssh client, yielding an empty string (and thus attempting to connect to `:22`) - doesn't matter whether `foo` is a hostname or IP address

Same issue here.  
When related host info is filled in `~/.ssh/config`, then I get the same "failed to connect to :22:" error.  
Commenting out the relevant line in `~/.ssh/config` allows podman to correctly establish connection, but now I have to type in the port manually everywhere else, where I use the defaults from `~/.ssh/config`

> I can reproduce this on 5.5.2
> 
> The situation seems to be as follows:
> 
> vscode adds a `.ssh/config` entry like:
> 
> ```
> Host foo
>     HostName foo
> ```
> 
> This then breaks the host lookup in podman's ssh client, yielding an empty string (and thus attempting to connect to `:22`) - doesn't matter whether `foo` is a hostname or IP address

i found a workaround first set the remote podman as default using this command `podman system connection default my-remote-machine` and then delete the `.ssh/config`  file in windows client after this when you verify the connection via `podman ps` it will work fine

Same issue here...


Windows podman version 5.7.0
Host podman version 5.6.0

When the hostname of host is entered in ~/.ssh/config as a host key
```
Host hostname.intra
  HostName hostname.intra
```

I receive error
``` PowerShell
PS> podman --url ssh://user@hostname.intra/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --identity C:\Users\user\.ssh\dev_ed25519 ps -a --log-level debug
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=info msg="C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe filtering at log level debug"
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe --url ssh://user@hostname.intra/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --identity C:\\Users\\user\\.ssh\\dev_ed25519 ps -a --log-level debug)"
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="ssh_config alias found: hostname.intra"
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="  User: user"
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="  Hostname: hostname.intra"
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="  Port: 22"
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="  IdentityFile: \"C:\\\\Users\\\\user\\\\.ssh\\\\dev_ed25519\""
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="SSH Ident Key \"C:\\\\Users\\\\user\\\\.ssh\\\\dev_ed25519\" <redacted> ssh-ed25519"
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp :22: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
time="2025-12-03T21:07:06+01:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

The same command succeeds as soon as I comment out the ~/ssh/config section
```
# Host hostname.intra
#   HostName hostname.intra
```

``` PowerShell
PS> podman --url ssh://user@hostname.intra/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --identity C:\Users\user\.ssh\dev_ed25519 ps --log-level debug
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=info msg="C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe filtering at log level debug"
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=debug msg="Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe --url ssh://user@hostname.intra/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --identity C:\\Users\\user\\.ssh\\dev_ed25519 ps --log-level debug)"
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=debug msg="SSH Ident Key \"C:\\\\Users\\\\user\\\\.ssh\\\\dev_ed25519\" <redacted> ssh-ed25519"
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.7.0/libpod/_ping"
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=debug msg="DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.7.0/libpod/containers/json"
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=debug msg="Called ps.PersistentPostRunE(C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe --url ssh://user@hostname.intra/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --identity C:\\Users\\user\\.ssh\\dev_ed25519 ps --log-level debug)"
time="2025-12-03T21:16:19+01:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

Username, UID and hostname were redacted/replaced with placeholders, but that should not affect anything...


Any updates?
